data.title: Is Strategy’s (MSTR) Bitcoin-Fueled Preferred Stock Turning It Into a Pure Crypto Proxy?
data.description:
• In recent days, Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) has continued to buy large amounts of Bitcoin, lifting its vast holdings toward profitability as Bitcoin rebounded on easing Middle East tensions and the reopening of key trade routes.
  • This rapid accumulation, funded partly through its Bitcoin-backed Stretch preferred stock, is turning Strategy into an increasingly pure proxy for Bitcoin exposure while reshaping how it positions yield-focused products for investors.

To own Strategy today, you really have to buy into its identity as a leveraged Bitcoin operating company more than a traditional software vendor. The recent US$2.6 billion buying spree, lifting holdings toward roughly 781,000 BTC and pushing the position back near breakeven as Bitcoin rebounded, only deepens that link. Short term, the key catalysts remain Bitcoin price moves, the Q1 2026 earnings print in May, and investor appetite for the Stretch/STRC preferred as a high-yield, Bitcoin-backed product. The proposal to shift STRC dividends to semi‑monthly and Saylor’s pitch of “bank accounts” earning up to 11.5% per year matter here, because they test whether yield-focused investors will keep funding this accumulation model. The flip side is clear: bigger Bitcoin exposure, higher dividend promises and recent stock volatility all increase sensitivity to any reversal in crypto sentiment or funding conditions.
